meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: China deployed over 100 vessels near Taiwan in the wake of Trump-Xi summit, Taiwan security official claims
items.summary: China deployed over 100 vessels around Taiwan after the Trump-Xi Beijing summit, Taiwan National Security Council Secretary General Joseph Wu says.

items.title: US embassy in Ukraine warns of 'potentially significant air attack' that could happen in next 24 hours
items.summary: The U.S. Embassy in Kyiv issued a security alert warning of a potentially significant air attack that may occur within the next 24 hours in Ukraine.

items.title: 'A new kind of war': Inside Ukraine's hidden factories mass-producing combat drones
items.summary: Get an exclusive look inside Ukraine's hidden drone factories rapidly producing kamikaze drones and interceptor systems to counter Iranian-designed Shahed and other Russian drones.

items.title: Ebola treatment center set on fire in Congo after residents clash with authorities over victim's body
items.summary: Angry residents reportedly set fire to an Ebola treatment center in eastern Congo during a clash over retrieving the body of a suspected Ebola victim.

items.title: US arrests sister of powerful Cuban official over alleged ties to communist regime
items.summary: U.S. arrests sister of Cuba's GAESA military conglomerate leader over alleged ties to the communist regime, Secretary of State Rubio announces.

items.title: Record number of climbers summit Mount Everest from Nepali side despite overcrowding concerns
items.summary: A record 274 climbers summited Mount Everest in a single day this week, raising fresh concerns about dangerous overcrowding on the world's tallest peak.

items.title: Two suspected American communist insurgents killed in clash in the Philippines
items.summary: Lyle Prijoles and Kai Dana-Rene Sorem were among 19 killed in a Philippine army firefight with suspected communist insurgents tied to the New People’s Army.

items.title: Mojtaba Khamenei using ‘bin Laden template’ to survive, learned from Abbottabad: analyst
items.summary: Analyst compares Mojtaba Khamenei's disappearance from public view to Osama bin Laden's decade of hiding, calling it a strategic milestone for the U.S.

items.title: Three sisters found dead in ocean at popular tourist beach as police probe mysterious tragedy
items.summary: Three sisters from London were formally identified after being found in the water near Brighton beach as police continue investigating their deaths.

items.title: Mamdani won't attend Israel Day Parade, breaking decades-long mayoral tradition amid antisemitism surge
items.summary: NYC's Israel Day Parade expects record turnout after Mayor Mamdani confirmed he will not attend, drawing criticism from Jewish leaders citywide.

items.title: 'Written in our DNA': Polish pilots who remember Soviet rule prepare for America's most lethal fighter jet
items.summary: Poland expects its first F-35 fighter jets very soon as U.S. and Polish forces train together at a key NATO air base near the alliance's eastern flank.

items.title: Hezbollah grooms children for martyrdom through its scout movement, report claims
items.summary: Experts say Hezbollah recruits and radicalizes youth through its Mahdi Scouts, preparing children to become fighters and carry out jihad missions.

items.title: WHO head 'deeply concerned' over 'scale and speed' of Ebola spread, says emergency committee will meet
items.summary: WHO chief convenes emergency committee over Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda, citing over 500 suspected cases and 131 fatalities from the virus.

items.title: American tourists arrested in Japan after alleged break-in at viral monkey Punch's enclosure
items.summary: Two Americans were reportedly arrested at Ichikawa City Zoo in Japan after one allegedly trespassed into viral monkey star Punch's enclosure this week.

items.title: Maduro ally deported to US over alleged billion-dollar corruption scheme tied to oil, food program
items.summary: Maduro ally Alex Saab faces federal charges and deportation to the U.S. for allegedly orchestrating a sweeping money laundering and bribery scheme.

items.title: Bodies of four missing Italian divers found inside 'shark cave' in Maldives days after they vanished
items.summary: Rescuers found four Italian divers' bodies deep inside a Maldives underwater cave days after they vanished during a dive far beyond recreational limits.

items.title: US, Nigeria strike ISIS fighters again from the air after killing senior leader
items.summary: U.S. and Nigerian forces struck ISIS targets in Nigeria days after President Trump announced the killing of global ISIS leader Abu-Bilal al-Minuki.

items.title: Canada confirms hantavirus case linked to cruise ship outbreak that has killed three passengers
items.summary: Canada confirms a hantavirus case linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship outbreak that has killed three people and sickened multiple passengers.

items.title: Netanyahu 'blunder' threatens US-backed Israel-UAE alliance at critical moment with Iran: analyst
items.summary: U.S.-brokered Israel-UAE alliance shows signs of strain after Netanyahu's claimed secret UAE visit drew a swift denial amid rising Iran tensions.

items.title: ISIS terror leader at large after US strike kills top commander amid rising Africa threat: analyst
items.summary: Human intelligence penetrated deep local networks to kill ISIS West Africa commander al-Minuki, described as the group's biggest loss in years.

items.title: NATO ally Poland warns Russia, Belarus pushing illegal migrants toward alliance — and the US
items.summary: Poland's military guards its Belarus border against what officials call a Russian-backed hybrid warfare campaign using migrants to destabilize NATO.

items.title: Maldives military diver dies searching for four Italian divers missing inside underwater cave system
items.summary: A Maldivian military diver died from decompression sickness during a perilous mission to recover bodies of four Italian divers trapped in a deep underwater cave.

items.title: King Charles 'shocked and saddened' over soldier's 'unexplained' death at horse show he attended
items.summary: King Charles was "shocked and saddened" after a soldier died following a fall at the Royal Windsor Horse Show during a King's Troop display Friday.

items.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in Central Africa a public health emergency after 80 suspected deaths
items.summary: The WHO declared the Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda an international public health emergency after dozens of suspected deaths were reported.

items.title: Man dies after attack by 13-foot great white shark near popular island resort: officials
items.summary: A 38-year-old man was killed in a shark attack near Rottnest Island off Australia's western coast, authorities say, urging additional caution in the area.
